ccel.title: ANF07. Fathers of the Third and Fourth Centuries: Lactantius, Venantius, Asterius, Victorinus, Dionysius, Apostolic Teaching and Constitutions, Homily, and Liturgies

source.title: ANF07. Fathers of the Third and Fourth Centuries: Lactantius, Venantius, Asterius, Victorinus, Dionysius, Apostolic Teaching and Constitutions, Homily, and Liturgies

title: Chapter XII.—Reception of Christians

source.title: ANF07. Fathers of the Third and Fourth Centuries: Lactantius, Venantius, Asterius, Victorinus, Dionysius, Apostolic Teaching and Constitutions, Homily, and Liturgies

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: But let every one that cometh in the name of the Lord be received, and afterward ye shall prove and know him; for ye shall have understanding right and left.

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: If he who cometh is a wayfarer, assist him as far as ye are able; but he shall not remain with you, except for two or three days, if need be.

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: But if he willeth to abide with you, being an artisan, let him work and eat; but if he hath no trade, 4. according to your understanding see to it that, as a Christian, he shall not live with you idle.

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: But if he willeth not to do, he is a Christ-monger.

verses.book: The Teaching of the Twelve Apostles
verses.text: Watch that ye keep aloof from such.
